text: This is the story of Babasaheb Nanabhau Pisore, a grassroots innovator from Beed, Maharashtra. Onion farmers in his area needed a variety with better shelf life and resistance to splitting and blotch disease than what was available. Through five years of mass selection from an existing variety, he developed Sona-40, an early-maturing onion with good shelf life and disease resistance, now distributed to farmers in a dozen states.

label.problem: Onion farmers in his area needed a variety with better shelf life and resistance to splitting and blotch disease than what was available.
label.solution: Through five years of mass selection from an existing variety, he developed Sona-40, an early-maturing onion with good shelf life and disease resistance, now distributed to farmers in a dozen states.

text: In Srinagar, Jammu & Kashmir, making a traditional Kashmiri namda woollen felt rug by hand, rolling and compressing wool for about an hour per piece, took a long time to complete even one. Zufa Iqbal responded with a practical fix: as a school student, she designed a motorised rolling machine with adjustable rollers that presses and fuses wool fibres into a finished namda in under ten minutes.

label.problem: Making a traditional Kashmiri namda woollen felt rug by hand, rolling and compressing wool for about an hour per piece, took a long time to complete even one.
label.solution: As a school student, she designed a motorised rolling machine with adjustable rollers that presses and fuses wool fibres into a finished namda in under ten minutes.

text: In Assam, riders on rough Assamese village roads had no way to get extra propulsion from a bicycle's motion beyond normal pedalling. Kanak Das responded with a practical fix: he designed a rider-induced bicycle mechanism that lets the rider's own body movement add extra drive to the cycle.

label.problem: Riders on rough Assamese village roads had no way to get extra propulsion from a bicycle's motion beyond normal pedalling.
label.solution: He designed a rider-induced bicycle mechanism that lets the rider's own body movement add extra drive to the cycle.

text: Ajaya Kumar Prusty, from Dhenkanal, Odisha, noticed that cutting paddy straw to the right size for mushroom beds by hand was slow and needed more than two workers per bed. To solve this, he built an adjustable straw cutter that trims paddy straw bundles to size quickly, and has sold over a thousand units across Odisha and Jharkhand.

label.problem: Cutting paddy straw to the right size for mushroom beds by hand was slow and needed more than two workers per bed.
label.solution: He built an adjustable straw cutter that trims paddy straw bundles to size quickly, and has sold over a thousand units across Odisha and Jharkhand.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — farmers growing several different crops needed separate threshing equipment for each one, which was expensive and inefficient for small landholders — Madanlal Kumawat came up with a grassroots solution. He built a multi-crop thresher that can process more than one type of crop, reducing the need to buy separate machines for each.

label.problem: Farmers growing several different crops needed separate threshing equipment for each one, which was expensive and inefficient for small landholders.
label.solution: He built a multi-crop thresher that can process more than one type of crop, reducing the need to buy separate machines for each.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — the local veterinary centre had no proper table for restraining and treating sick animals, forcing multiple people to hold a distressed animal down, and the centre itself was often far away over poor roads — Imna Meren came up with a grassroots solution. He built a hydraulically adjustable wooden operation table for small animals with straps, an IV stand, and drainage, letting basic veterinary procedures be done safely closer to home.

label.problem: The local veterinary centre had no proper table for restraining and treating sick animals, forcing multiple people to hold a distressed animal down, and the centre itself was often far away over poor roads.
label.solution: He built a hydraulically adjustable wooden operation table for small animals with straps, an IV stand, and drainage, letting basic veterinary procedures be done safely closer to home.

text: In Kishtiwar, Jammu & Kashmir, the traditional Kashmiri bukhari room heater burned through 3 to 4 kilograms of wood or coal an hour and retained heat for only about half an hour after the fire went out. Towseef Ali Malik responded with a practical fix: he modified the bukhari with gypsum and baked-clay logs that retain heat far longer, cutting fuel use to under a kilogram an hour while holding warmth for three to four hours.

label.problem: The traditional Kashmiri bukhari room heater burned through 3 to 4 kilograms of wood or coal an hour and retained heat for only about half an hour after the fire went out.
label.solution: He modified the bukhari with gypsum and baked-clay logs that retain heat far longer, cutting fuel use to under a kilogram an hour while holding warmth for three to four hours.

text: This is the story of P. A. Sekar, a grassroots innovator from Thiruvallur, Tamil Nadu. Sizing threads before weaving was traditionally done by hand every 19 metres, a slow, labour-intensive step in saree production. He built a machine that sizes threads over much longer 200-metre runs, multiplying daily output roughly twelvefold for the weaving families who use it.

label.problem: Sizing threads before weaving was traditionally done by hand every 19 metres, a slow, labour-intensive step in saree production.
label.solution: He built a machine that sizes threads over much longer 200-metre runs, multiplying daily output roughly twelvefold for the weaving families who use it.

text: A schoolgirl in rural Kerala had to spend hours each day hand-washing clothes for her family while also managing her studies and a sick parent, with no washing machine affordable or available. That was the situation Remya Jose set out to change in Palakkad, Kerala. She designed a low-cost pedal-powered washing machine that uses foot power instead of electricity, cutting washing time and effort for households without power or money for an electric machine.

label.problem: A schoolgirl in rural Kerala had to spend hours each day hand-washing clothes for her family while also managing her studies and a sick parent, with no washing machine affordable or available.
label.solution: She designed a low-cost pedal-powered washing machine that uses foot power instead of electricity, cutting washing time and effort for households without power or money for an electric machine.

text: Livestock owners had no reliable way to help cows and buffaloes expel the placenta and complete uterine recovery quickly after calving, risking the animal's health. That was the situation Shishpal Singh set out to change in Meerut, Uttar Pradesh. He developed a herbal medication that, when administered after calving, speeds up expulsion of the placenta and involution of the uterus in the animal.

label.problem: Livestock owners had no reliable way to help cows and buffaloes expel the placenta and complete uterine recovery quickly after calving, risking the animal's health.
label.solution: He developed a herbal medication that, when administered after calving, speeds up expulsion of the placenta and involution of the uterus in the animal.

text: Farmers needing to move a water pump between different small, scattered plots found fixed pump installations inconvenient. That was the situation Nasiruddin Gayen set out to change in West Bengal. He built a bicycle-based portable pump that can be wheeled to wherever water is needed and operated by pedalling.

label.problem: Farmers needing to move a water pump between different small, scattered plots found fixed pump installations inconvenient.
label.solution: He built a bicycle-based portable pump that can be wheeled to wherever water is needed and operated by pedalling.

text: The traditional Kashmiri kangri fire pot can spill hot embers and cause burns if it tilts or falls. That was the situation Asim Sikander Mir set out to change in Kulgam, Jammu & Kashmir. As a school student, he designed a 'gyroscopic' kangri with an inner spinning pot that keeps its orientation and stays upright even if the outer pot tilts, preventing embers from spilling.

label.problem: The traditional Kashmiri kangri fire pot can spill hot embers and cause burns if it tilts or falls.
label.solution: As a school student, he designed a 'gyroscopic' kangri with an inner spinning pot that keeps its orientation and stays upright even if the outer pot tilts, preventing embers from spilling.

text: Transplanting paddy seedlings became increasingly expensive as labour wages rose and workers grew scarce during the peak season. That was the situation Shyambir Singh and Ved Prakash set out to change in Palwal, Haryana. The two friends designed an engine-powered paddy transplanter, later modified to run on tractor power, that transplants up to nine rows of seedlings at once.

label.problem: Transplanting paddy seedlings became increasingly expensive as labour wages rose and workers grew scarce during the peak season.
label.solution: The two friends designed an engine-powered paddy transplanter, later modified to run on tractor power, that transplants up to nine rows of seedlings at once.

text: This is the story of Rajmani Singh and Mukesh Kumar Singh, a grassroots innovator from Vaishali, Bihar. Farmers growing traditional cauliflower cultivars in their district lacked an early-maturing, high-yielding variety with good curd quality. Through years of selecting and multiplying seed from a traditional cauliflower variety, the brothers developed Sonali-45, an early, high-yielding cauliflower with compact white curds, and went on to sell the seed commercially.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — winding cotton thread by hand for weaving traditional Sambalpuri sarees was slow, and skilled labour for the job was becoming harder to find — Prafulla Kumar Meher came up with a grassroots solution. text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — cows and buffaloes affected by anestrus fail to enter their breeding cycle, limiting a farmer's ability to grow their herd — Vilat Yadav came up with a grassroots solution. He developed a herbal powder treatment that, mixed in water and given twice daily, brought most treated animals into oestrus within about two weeks in validation trials.

text: Subhas Vasantrao Jagtap, from Maharashtra, noticed that farmers spraying crops manually struggled to carry pump equipment across large plots without help. text: Temples that need a continuous supply of pure sandalwood paste for religious rituals relied on the slow, laborious process of grinding it by hand. A veteran fabricator, he built a motorised rotary grinder that produces smooth sandalwood paste from small logs, saving temples the manual grinding work.

text: In Dharwad, Karnataka, small farmers sowing seed and applying fertiliser separately spent extra time and often placed fertiliser inconsistently relative to the seed. text: Farmers in his hill district had no quick remedy for livestock suffering from bloat. That was the situation Vijay Ram set out to change in Nainital, Uttarakhand. text: This is the story of Mohammad Aminuddin, a grassroots innovator from Kolkata, West Bengal. Tarpaulin shop shelters in narrow, low-lying bylanes collected rainwater during monsoon storms, becoming heavy, unstable, and prone to collapse or spilling water on customers. He built a tarpaulin shed with a built-in drain channel that carries collected rainwater off the roof and into the street drain, and has sold thousands of units to fellow shopkeepers.

label.problem: Farmers preparing hard, clod-filled soil for planting had no efficient way to break up clods and level uneven wetland fields in one pass.
label.solution: He built a cultivator-mounted soil clod crusher and a tractor-mounted wetland leveller that break up soil clods, bury weeds for mulching, and level fields for planting.

text: Peeling pineapples by hand in his hilly district was a careful, time-consuming task. That was the situation Imkongsunep set out to change in Mokokchung, Nagaland. He built a small lathe-like machine that mounts and rotates a pineapple while a blade peels it in a few seconds, adjustable to different pineapple sizes.

label.problem: Peeling pineapples by hand in his hilly district was a careful, time-consuming task.
label.solution: He built a small lathe-like machine that mounts and rotates a pineapple while a blade peels it in a few seconds, adjustable to different pineapple sizes.

text: Rajmani Singh and Mukesh Kumar Singh, from Vaishali, Bihar, noticed that farmers growing traditional cauliflower cultivars in their district lacked an early-maturing, high-yielding variety with good curd quality. To solve this, through years of selecting and multiplying seed from a traditional cauliflower variety, the brothers developed Sonali-45, an early, high-yielding cauliflower with compact white curds, and went on to sell the seed commercially.

label.problem: Farmers growing traditional cauliflower cultivars in their district lacked an early-maturing, high-yielding variety with good curd quality.
label.solution: Through years of selecting and multiplying seed from a traditional cauliflower variety, the brothers developed Sonali-45, an early, high-yielding cauliflower with compact white curds, and went on to sell the seed commercially.

text: Folding and binding V-shaped silkworm breeding nets by hand between rearing cycles was slow, limiting workers to about 200 nets a day. That was the situation Sunil Ajurnarav Shinde set out to change in Jalna, Maharashtra. He built a pipe-frame pressing device that folds and holds nets in place so they can be tied quickly, raising output to 300 nets an hour.

label.problem: Folding and binding V-shaped silkworm breeding nets by hand between rearing cycles was slow, limiting workers to about 200 nets a day.
label.solution: He built a pipe-frame pressing device that folds and holds nets in place so they can be tied quickly, raising output to 300 nets an hour.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — small-scale processing setups were wasting the heat given off during condensation, adding unnecessary fuel cost — Subhash Ola came up with a grassroots solution. label.problem: Small-scale processing setups were wasting the heat given off during condensation, adding unnecessary fuel cost.
label.solution: He built a condensate and heat recovery system that captures and reuses this waste heat, cutting fuel costs for the process.

text: Conventional steel bicycle frames were costly for poor rural users in Assam, where bamboo is abundant and cheap. That was the situation Dodhi Pathak set out to change in Assam. He built a bicycle with a bamboo frame, using a locally available low-cost material in place of steel to bring down the price of the cycle.

label.problem: Conventional steel bicycle frames were costly for poor rural users in Assam, where bamboo is abundant and cheap.
label.solution: He built a bicycle with a bamboo frame, using a locally available low-cost material in place of steel to bring down the price of the cycle.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — tarpaulin shop shelters in narrow, low-lying bylanes collected rainwater during monsoon storms, becoming heavy, unstable, and prone to collapse or spilling water on customers — Mohammad Aminuddin came up with a grassroots solution. He built a tarpaulin shed with a built-in drain channel that carries collected rainwater off the roof and into the street drain, and has sold thousands of units to fellow shopkeepers.

text: Subhash Vasantrao Jagtap, from Jalgaon, Maharashtra, noticed that temples that need a continuous supply of pure sandalwood paste for religious rituals relied on the slow, laborious process of grinding it by hand. To solve this, a veteran fabricator, he built a motorised rotary grinder that produces smooth sandalwood paste from small logs, saving temples the manual grinding work.

label.problem: Temples that need a continuous supply of pure sandalwood paste for religious rituals relied on the slow, laborious process of grinding it by hand.

text: Sayen Akhtar Sheikh, from South Andaman, Andaman & Nicobar Islands, noticed that removing the plastic cap on an LPG cylinder nozzle required pulling a nylon thread clip and the cap at the same time, and the thread often cut the user's fingers. To solve this, after seeing a family member get hurt, this school student built an opener that pulls the clip lock and lifts the cap in one motion.

label.problem: Removing the plastic cap on an LPG cylinder nozzle required pulling a nylon thread clip and the cap at the same time, and the thread often cut the user's fingers.

text: Dipak Sardar, from West Bengal, noticed that producing sola (shola) pith wood sheets by hand for craft and decoration was slow and labour-intensive. label.problem: Producing sola (shola) pith wood sheets by hand for craft and decoration was slow and labour-intensive.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — peeling green walnuts by hand right after harvest was slow, tiring, and damaged the skin of workers' hands — Mohd. text: This is the story of Dhanjibhai Kerai, a grassroots innovator from Gujarat. As a physically challenged person himself, Dhanjibhai found that standard scooters were unusable for people with limited mobility, leaving them with few affordable transport options. He modified an existing scooter with hand-operated controls so that physically challenged riders can operate it safely, giving them independent mobility.

label.problem: As a physically challenged person himself, Dhanjibhai found that standard scooters were unusable for people with limited mobility, leaving them with few affordable transport options.

text: Harvesting coconuts required climbing trees using ropes or bare feet, a risky task made harder by a shrinking pool of skilled tree climbers, and separating pupae from Eri silk cocoons by boiling damaged yarn quality. He built a handheld, finger-gripped tool on a bamboo pole that plucks selected coconuts from the ground without climbing, and a separate device that opens Eri cocoons without boiling, preserving yarn quality.

text: Nileshbhai and Pankaj, from Gujarat, noticed that collecting harvested and dried groundnut stalks from the field by hand delayed processing and caused losses when labour was scarce. label.problem: Collecting harvested and dried groundnut stalks from the field by hand delayed processing and caused losses when labour was scarce.

text: In India (region not confirmed), planting turmeric and ginger by hand at consistent spacing was slow and required significant manual labour. Indrajit Singh Khass responded with a practical fix: he built a tractor-mounted turmeric and ginger planter with adjustable row spacing that opens the furrow, meters the seed, and places it automatically.

label.problem: Planting turmeric and ginger by hand at consistent spacing was slow and required significant manual labour.

text: Desai Laxmanbhai Devkaranbhai, from Aravalli, Gujarat, noticed that livestock affected by bloat suffer painful abdominal swelling and reduced organ motility, and farmers had limited treatment options. text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — small farmers without electricity connections had no affordable way to lift water from shallow sources to irrigate small plots — Vikram Rathore came up with a grassroots solution. He built a bicycle-operated water pump, so pedalling draws water for irrigation without needing electricity or a diesel engine.

label.problem: Small farmers without electricity connections had no affordable way to lift water from shallow sources to irrigate small plots.

text: In Tiruvannamalai, Tamil Nadu, weavers operating Jacquard handlooms had to manually lift a heavy Jacquard box hundreds of times a day using a leg and wooden lever, causing physical strain and lower productivity. P Ravi responded with a practical fix: he designed a motorised attachment that lifts the Jacquard box at the press of a pedal, and has sold thousands of units to handloom weavers.

label.problem: Weavers operating Jacquard handlooms had to manually lift a heavy Jacquard box hundreds of times a day using a leg and wooden lever, causing physical strain and lower productivity.

text: In Bareilly, Uttar Pradesh, as a physically challenged homemaker, she found that standard crutches slipped on wet or smooth floors and tended to slide out from under her arm. Durga Devi responded with a practical fix: she modified ordinary crutches with a shoulder belt, a shock absorber, and a vacuum-cup base that grips smooth or wet flooring, preventing slips.

text: This is the story of Remya Jose, a grassroots innovator from Palakkad, Kerala. She designed a low-cost pedal-powered washing machine that uses foot power instead of electricity, cutting washing time and effort for households without power or money for an electric machine.

Fabricators cutting and bending iron bars with a standard cut-off saw had to replace the abrasive wheel frequently and endure loud noise during the job. text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — weaving bamboo baskets by hand let a weaver make only three or four baskets a day, keeping their earnings low — Sumit Murari came up with a grassroots solution. text: Leimapokpam Helendro Singh, from Imphal West, Manipur, noticed that farmers growing traditional Manipuri chilli varieties had to choose between a tastier, low-yielding variety and a higher-yielding one with smaller fruit. To solve this, by selecting unusually large fruit from a high-yielding traditional variety over ten years, he developed the Helen Morok chilli, which combines good yield, big fruit, and disease tolerance.

label.problem: Farmers growing traditional Manipuri chilli varieties had to choose between a tastier, low-yielding variety and a higher-yielding one with smaller fruit.
label.solution: By selecting unusually large fruit from a high-yielding traditional variety over ten years, he developed the Helen Morok chilli, which combines good yield, big fruit, and disease tolerance.

text: Vijay Ram, from Nainital, Uttarakhand, noticed that farmers in his hill district had no quick remedy for livestock suffering from bloat. To solve this, using a herbal treatment learned from family elders, he developed a dried-herb powder dose that relieves bloat symptoms in animals within a few hours.

label.problem: Farmers in his hill district had no quick remedy for livestock suffering from bloat.
label.solution: Using a herbal treatment learned from family elders, he developed a dried-herb powder dose that relieves bloat symptoms in animals within a few hours.

text: This is the story of Nasiruddin Gayen, a grassroots innovator from West Bengal. Farmers needing to move a water pump between different small, scattered plots found fixed pump installations inconvenient. He built a bicycle-based portable pump that can be wheeled to wherever water is needed and operated by pedalling.

label.problem: Farmers needing to move a water pump between different small, scattered plots found fixed pump installations inconvenient.
label.solution: He built a bicycle-based portable pump that can be wheeled to wherever water is needed and operated by pedalling.

text: Rajanikant Raghavjibhai Dhedhi, from Morbi, Gujarat, noticed that farmers had to attach and detach separate cultivator and blade-harrow implements for ploughing and harrowing, wasting time between operations. To solve this, he built a combined implement that carries both a cultivator and a blade harrow on a reversible mount, letting a farmer switch between the two operations without swapping equipment.

label.problem: Farmers had to attach and detach separate cultivator and blade-harrow implements for ploughing and harrowing, wasting time between operations.
label.solution: He built a combined implement that carries both a cultivator and a blade harrow on a reversible mount, letting a farmer switch between the two operations without swapping equipment.

text: In Ratnagiri, Maharashtra, turmeric farmers in his area lacked a variety that combined early maturity, high yield, and resistance to rhizome rot disease. Sachin Kamlakar Karekar responded with a practical fix: through years of selecting the best-performing plants from a local variety, he developed the SK-4 turmeric variety, which matures early, yields more, and tolerates rhizome rot better than standard varieties.

label.problem: Turmeric farmers in his area lacked a variety that combined early maturity, high yield, and resistance to rhizome rot disease.
label.solution: Through years of selecting the best-performing plants from a local variety, he developed the SK-4 turmeric variety, which matures early, yields more, and tolerates rhizome rot better than standard varieties.

text: Nannem Tirupathi Rao, from Prakasham, Andhra Pradesh, noticed that as an electrician who installs power lines, he found climbing cement electric poles especially difficult and risky compared to wooden ones. To solve this, he designed a bent steel-rod climbing frame fitted with sandals that lets a worker climb any type of electric pole safely, tested to hold up to 150 kilograms.

label.problem: As an electrician who installs power lines, he found climbing cement electric poles especially difficult and risky compared to wooden ones.
label.solution: He designed a bent steel-rod climbing frame fitted with sandals that lets a worker climb any type of electric pole safely, tested to hold up to 150 kilograms.

text: This is the story of Mohammad Saidullah, a grassroots innovator from Bihar. People in flood-prone parts of Bihar had no easy way to commute once roads and low areas were submerged, cutting them off during the monsoon. He converted an ordinary bicycle into an amphibious version that can also move across water, letting people cross flooded roads, ponds, and small water bodies during floods.

label.solution: He converted an ordinary bicycle into an amphibious version that can also move across water, letting people cross flooded roads, ponds, and small water bodies during floods.

text: In Kullu, Himachal Pradesh, insects and termites were damaging fruit orchards and vegetable crops on her farm. Divya Sharma responded with a practical fix: she developed a fermented preparation from the fruit of a local tree that protects apples, pears, plums, and vegetables from insects and termites.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — rural households and small farms needed an affordable source of fuel gas but had limited access to conventional cooking or power fuel — Rai Singh Dahiya came up with a grassroots solution. He built a biomass gasifier system that converts crop residue and organic waste into usable gas fuel.

label.problem: Rural households and small farms needed an affordable source of fuel gas but had limited access to conventional cooking or power fuel.
label.solution: He built a biomass gasifier system that converts crop residue and organic waste into usable gas fuel.

text: Harvesting and washing taro by hand with a spade took many labourers and days of work, driving up costs for farmers. That was the situation Yanglem Brajamani Singh set out to change in Bishnupur, Manipur. He built a tractor-mounted taro harvester with an attached washer that digs up and cleans the crop, cutting a job that took a week down to about a day.

label.problem: Harvesting and washing taro by hand with a spade took many labourers and days of work, driving up costs for farmers.

text: Sundaram Verma, from Rajasthan, noticed that farmers in his arid region struggled to grow crops reliably given scarce and unpredictable rainfall. To solve this, he carried out decades of on-farm research into dryland cropping and water-conservation techniques, developing practices that help farmers grow crops with very little water, and went on to scout and mentor several other grassroots innovators for NIF.

text: In Leh, Ladakh, sea buckthorn's thorny branches make hand-harvesting its berries slow and painful, and many berries go unpicked because they're out of reach. Tsering Omphel responded with a practical fix: he designed a low-cost harvesting device that plucks sea buckthorn berries without requiring bare-handed picking, reducing injury and fruit wastage.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — many poor rural households in Gujarat had no electricity connection or could not afford a refrigerator, leaving them without a way to keep food and drinking water cool — Mansukhbhai Prajapati came up with a grassroots solution. A potter by trade, he designed 'Mitticool,' a refrigerator made entirely of unglazed clay that cools its contents through natural evaporation and needs no electricity to run.

text: In South Andaman, Andaman & Nicobar Islands, removing the plastic cap on an LPG cylinder nozzle required pulling a nylon thread clip and the cap at the same time, and the thread often cut the user's fingers. Sayen Akhtar Sheikh responded with a practical fix: after seeing a family member get hurt, this school student built an opener that pulls the clip lock and lifts the cap in one motion.

label.problem: Separating tamarind seeds from the pulp by hand was slow, tedious work for processors and farmers.
label.solution: He built a device that mechanically separates tamarind seeds from pulp, speeding up processing for a crop with limited mechanisation.

text: Livestock in her village regularly suffered from bloat, and farmers had few treatment options on hand. That was the situation Laxmiben Pratapji Thakarda set out to change in Banaskantha, Gujarat. A recognised village veterinary healer, she developed a herbal leaf remedy that relieves bloat symptoms in animals within hours of dosing.

label.problem: Livestock in her village regularly suffered from bloat, and farmers had few treatment options on hand.
label.solution: A recognised village veterinary healer, she developed a herbal leaf remedy that relieves bloat symptoms in animals within hours of dosing.

text: Mansukhbhai Prajapati, from Wankaner, Gujarat, noticed that many poor rural households in Gujarat had no electricity connection or could not afford a refrigerator, leaving them without a way to keep food and drinking water cool. To solve this, a potter by trade, he designed 'Mitticool,' a refrigerator made entirely of unglazed clay that cools its contents through natural evaporation and needs no electricity to run.

label.problem: Many poor rural households in Gujarat had no electricity connection or could not afford a refrigerator, leaving them without a way to keep food and drinking water cool.
label.solution: A potter by trade, he designed 'Mitticool,' a refrigerator made entirely of unglazed clay that cools its contents through natural evaporation and needs no electricity to run.

text: Small farmers without electricity connections had no affordable way to lift water from shallow sources to irrigate small plots. That was the situation Vikram Rathore set out to change in Andhra Pradesh. He built a bicycle-operated water pump, so pedalling draws water for irrigation without needing electricity or a diesel engine.

label.problem: Small farmers without electricity connections had no affordable way to lift water from shallow sources to irrigate small plots.
label.solution: He built a bicycle-operated water pump, so pedalling draws water for irrigation without needing electricity or a diesel engine.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — standard life jackets keep a person afloat but provide no way to breathe if they need oxygen in a drowning emergency — Mauwang Wangham came up with a grassroots solution. After watching a friend drown, he proposed a life jacket fitted with an attached oxygen mask for use in water emergencies.

label.problem: Standard life jackets keep a person afloat but provide no way to breathe if they need oxygen in a drowning emergency.
label.solution: After watching a friend drown, he proposed a life jacket fitted with an attached oxygen mask for use in water emergencies.

text: Jitabhai Kodarbhai Patel, from Sabarkantha, Gujarat, noticed that farmers growing hyacinth bean (val) lacked an early-maturing, high-yielding variety with good taste for the local Undhiyu dish market. To solve this, through eight years of selecting superior plants found mixed into collected fodder, he developed the JK-1 hyacinth bean variety, prized for its high yield and taste.

label.problem: Farmers growing hyacinth bean (val) lacked an early-maturing, high-yielding variety with good taste for the local Undhiyu dish market.
label.solution: Through eight years of selecting superior plants found mixed into collected fodder, he developed the JK-1 hyacinth bean variety, prized for its high yield and taste.

text: This is the story of Jyotsna Mayee Patra, a grassroots innovator from Keonjhar, Odisha. Insect infestations were damaging rose plants and vegetable and rice crops in her home garden and fields. After noticing a local climber plant's decaying fruit kept insects away from nearby roses, she developed a preparation from the same plant that controls insect pests in vegetables and rice when applied to the soil.

label.problem: Insect infestations were damaging rose plants and vegetable and rice crops in her home garden and fields.
label.solution: After noticing a local climber plant's decaying fruit kept insects away from nearby roses, she developed a preparation from the same plant that controls insect pests in vegetables and rice when applied to the soil.

text: Zufa Iqbal, from Srinagar, Jammu & Kashmir, noticed that making a traditional Kashmiri namda woollen felt rug by hand, rolling and compressing wool for about an hour per piece, took a long time to complete even one. To solve this, as a school student, she designed a motorised rolling machine with adjustable rollers that presses and fuses wool fibres into a finished namda in under ten minutes.

label.problem: Making a traditional Kashmiri namda woollen felt rug by hand, rolling and compressing wool for about an hour per piece, took a long time to complete even one.
label.solution: As a school student, she designed a motorised rolling machine with adjustable rollers that presses and fuses wool fibres into a finished namda in under ten minutes.

text: In Namakkal, Tamil Nadu, existing machines for moulding areca leaves into disposable plates had drawbacks that made them hard to maintain and physically demanding to operate, especially for women. Chinnakannu Muthusami responded with a practical fix: he redesigned the leaf-moulding machine with a hydraulic mechanism and paired moulds, making it easier to operate and able to produce around 1,700 plates in an eight-hour day.

label.problem: Existing machines for moulding areca leaves into disposable plates had drawbacks that made them hard to maintain and physically demanding to operate, especially for women.
label.solution: He redesigned the leaf-moulding machine with a hydraulic mechanism and paired moulds, making it easier to operate and able to produce around 1,700 plates in an eight-hour day.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — farmers in Gujarat lacked a carrot variety suited to local soil and climate that was also highly nutritious — Vallabhbhai Marvaniya came up with a grassroots solution. He developed and popularised the Madhuban Gajar carrot variety, a locally adapted, nutrient-rich carrot that farmers in the region could grow successfully.

label.problem: Farmers in Gujarat lacked a carrot variety suited to local soil and climate that was also highly nutritious.
label.solution: He developed and popularised the Madhuban Gajar carrot variety, a locally adapted, nutrient-rich carrot that farmers in the region could grow successfully.

text: Divya Sharma, from Kullu, Himachal Pradesh, noticed that insects and termites were damaging fruit orchards and vegetable crops on her farm. To solve this, she developed a fermented preparation from the fruit of a local tree that protects apples, pears, plums, and vegetables from insects and termites.

text: This is the story of Kanak Das, a grassroots innovator from Assam. Riders on rough Assamese village roads had no way to get extra propulsion from a bicycle's motion beyond normal pedalling. He designed a rider-induced bicycle mechanism that lets the rider's own body movement add extra drive to the cycle.

label.problem: Riders on rough Assamese village roads had no way to get extra propulsion from a bicycle's motion beyond normal pedalling.
label.solution: He designed a rider-induced bicycle mechanism that lets the rider's own body movement add extra drive to the cycle.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — farmers needed a cauliflower variety that could better withstand local pests and climate stress, since standard varieties often failed under these conditions — Jagdish Prasad Parikh came up with a grassroots solution. He developed a pest- and climate-resistant cauliflower variety that performs more reliably for farmers in tough growing conditions.

label.problem: Farmers needed a cauliflower variety that could better withstand local pests and climate stress, since standard varieties often failed under these conditions.
label.solution: He developed a pest- and climate-resistant cauliflower variety that performs more reliably for farmers in tough growing conditions.

label.problem: Livestock in her village regularly suffered from bloat, and farmers had few treatment options on hand.

text: After losing his nutmeg orchard to disease, he needed a new, higher-quality nutmeg variety to rebuild his farm's income. That was the situation E. V. Thomas set out to change in Idukki, Kerala. By propagating and selecting plants with an unusual lateral branching pattern that made harvesting easier, he developed the Edavarembil Gold nutmeg variety, prized for its high oil content and consistent yield.

label.problem: After losing his nutmeg orchard to disease, he needed a new, higher-quality nutmeg variety to rebuild his farm's income.
label.solution: By propagating and selecting plants with an unusual lateral branching pattern that made harvesting easier, he developed the Edavarembil Gold nutmeg variety, prized for its high oil content and consistent yield.

text: Akhmabhai Vagadia, from Mahisagar, Gujarat, noticed that cows and buffaloes affected by anestrus fail to enter their reproductive cycle, hurting a farmer's ability to breed and maintain their herd. To solve this, he developed a herbal medication that, when administered to affected animals, helps resume normal ovarian cycling and improves conception rates.

label.problem: Cows and buffaloes affected by anestrus fail to enter their reproductive cycle, hurting a farmer's ability to breed and maintain their herd.
label.solution: He developed a herbal medication that, when administered to affected animals, helps resume normal ovarian cycling and improves conception rates.

text: This is the story of Karappan Venkatraman, a grassroots innovator from Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu. When a chikungunya outbreak left many local handloom weavers with severe joint pain, they could no longer operate looms that needed both legs and hands to run. He designed a handloom that can be woven using just one leg and no hands, letting weavers affected by joint pain keep working.

label.problem: When a chikungunya outbreak left many local handloom weavers with severe joint pain, they could no longer operate looms that needed both legs and hands to run.
label.solution: He designed a handloom that can be woven using just one leg and no hands, letting weavers affected by joint pain keep working.

text: In Kullu, Himachal Pradesh, insect pests were damaging wheat, barley, and vegetable crops for farmers in his district, cutting into their harvests. Roshan Lal responded with a practical fix: he developed a decoction from local plant bark, leaves, and fruit that controls sucking and borer insects in crops when used at low doses.

label.problem: Insect pests were damaging wheat, barley, and vegetable crops for farmers in his district, cutting into their harvests.
label.solution: He developed a decoction from local plant bark, leaves, and fruit that controls sucking and borer insects in crops when used at low doses.

text: The local veterinary centre had no proper table for restraining and treating sick animals, forcing multiple people to hold a distressed animal down, and the centre itself was often far away over poor roads. That was the situation Imna Meren set out to change in Dimapur, Nagaland. He built a hydraulically adjustable wooden operation table for small animals with straps, an IV stand, and drainage, letting basic veterinary procedures be done safely closer to home.

label.problem: The local veterinary centre had no proper table for restraining and treating sick animals, forcing multiple people to hold a distressed animal down, and the centre itself was often far away over poor roads.
label.solution: He built a hydraulically adjustable wooden operation table for small animals with straps, an IV stand, and drainage, letting basic veterinary procedures be done safely closer to home.

text: In Uttar Pradesh, farmers wanted a higher-quality, better-yielding guava variety suited to local growing conditions. Ram Vilas Maurya responded with a practical fix: he developed the 'G-Vilas Pasand' improved guava variety, giving farmers a better-performing option to cultivate.

label.problem: Farmers wanted a higher-quality, better-yielding guava variety suited to local growing conditions.
label.solution: He developed the 'G-Vilas Pasand' improved guava variety, giving farmers a better-performing option to cultivate.

text: This is the story of Dinesh Chaudhary, a grassroots innovator from Durg, Chhattisgarh. A local farmer needed a way to dig planting channels for banana cultivation without the slow, labour-heavy process of doing it by hand. He built a tractor-operated channel-making machine that digs and moves soil with rotating blades, a design now also used for papaya cultivation.

label.problem: A local farmer needed a way to dig planting channels for banana cultivation without the slow, labour-heavy process of doing it by hand.
label.solution: He built a tractor-operated channel-making machine that digs and moves soil with rotating blades, a design now also used for papaya cultivation.

text: This is the story of Punamchand Patidar, a grassroots innovator from Jhalawar, Rajasthan. Local farmers needed a higher-yielding onion variety with good shelf life suited to their region. Through selection from a traditional variety, he developed the Kansi No.1 onion, a high-yielding, disease-free variety with good shelf life that he distributed through farmer networks.

text: In Dhemaji, Assam, harvesting coconuts required climbing trees using ropes or bare feet, a risky task made harder by a shrinking pool of skilled tree climbers, and separating pupae from Eri silk cocoons by boiling damaged yarn quality. Budheswar Pamey responded with a practical fix: he built a handheld, finger-gripped tool on a bamboo pole that plucks selected coconuts from the ground without climbing, and a separate device that opens Eri cocoons without boiling, preserving yarn quality.

label.problem: Harvesting coconuts required climbing trees using ropes or bare feet, a risky task made harder by a shrinking pool of skilled tree climbers, and separating pupae from Eri silk cocoons by boiling damaged yarn quality.

text: In Beed, Maharashtra, onion farmers in his area needed a variety with better shelf life and resistance to splitting and blotch disease than what was available. Babasaheb Nanabhau Pisore responded with a practical fix: through five years of mass selection from an existing variety, he developed Sona-40, an early-maturing onion with good shelf life and disease resistance, now distributed to farmers in a dozen states.

label.problem: Onion farmers in his area needed a variety with better shelf life and resistance to splitting and blotch disease than what was available.
label.solution: Through five years of mass selection from an existing variety, he developed Sona-40, an early-maturing onion with good shelf life and disease resistance, now distributed to farmers in a dozen states.

text: This is the story of Shamalbhai Kanabhai Gamar, a grassroots innovator from Sabarkantha, Gujarat. Dairy animals with mastitis suffered painful, inflamed udders and bloody milk, with limited treatment options for farmers in his village. Using a remedy passed down from his father, he developed a herbal treatment administered directly into the udder that reduces inflammation and curtails bacterial infection over about ten days.

label.problem: Dairy animals with mastitis suffered painful, inflamed udders and bloody milk, with limited treatment options for farmers in his village.

text: In Nadia, West Bengal, he wanted to build his newborn daughter a swing that could adapt safely to her needs as she grew, from infant to toddler to young child. Sapan Kumar Mandal responded with a practical fix: he designed a rail-guided sliding swing that can be reconfigured over time, from an infant's bed to a rod-protected toddler seat to a backed seat for an older child.

label.problem: He wanted to build his newborn daughter a swing that could adapt safely to her needs as she grew, from infant to toddler to young child.
label.solution: He designed a rail-guided sliding swing that can be reconfigured over time, from an infant's bed to a rod-protected toddler seat to a backed seat for an older child.

text: This is the story of Anang Tadar, a grassroots innovator from Papum Pare, Arunachal Pradesh. Visually impaired people can detect obstacles at ground level with a cane, but had no way to sense obstacles at waist height and above while walking. As a student, he built smart goggles fitted with ultrasonic sensors that detect obstacles ahead and alert the wearer with vibrations, complementing a blind cane rather than replacing it.

text: Prakash Singh Raghuvanshi, from Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, noticed that farmers lacked access to a range of reliable, improved plant varieties suited to their local growing conditions. text: In Palakkad, Kerala, a schoolgirl in rural Kerala had to spend hours each day hand-washing clothes for her family while also managing her studies and a sick parent, with no washing machine affordable or available. Remya Jose responded with a practical fix: she designed a low-cost pedal-powered washing machine that uses foot power instead of electricity, cutting washing time and effort for households without power or money for an electric machine.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — sea buckthorn's thorny branches make hand-harvesting its berries slow and painful, and many berries go unpicked because they're out of reach — Tsering Omphel came up with a grassroots solution. text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — conventional steel bicycle frames were costly for poor rural users in Assam, where bamboo is abundant and cheap — Dodhi Pathak came up with a grassroots solution. He built a bicycle with a bamboo frame, using a locally available low-cost material in place of steel to bring down the price of the cycle.

label.problem: Conventional steel bicycle frames were costly for poor rural users in Assam, where bamboo is abundant and cheap.

text: Traditional Hindu cremation burns around 400 kilograms of wood per body, consuming millions of tonnes of wood nationally each year. That was the situation Arjunbhai M. Paghdar set out to change in Junagadh, Gujarat. He designed a biomass-gasification cremation structure lined with heat-retaining refractory brick and cera-wool that cremates a body using only 70 to 80 kilograms of wood in under half the usual time.

text: Suren Barua, from Tinsukia, Assam, noticed that people without access to air conditioning struggled to stay comfortable during hot summer nights, especially the sick and elderly who spend long periods in bed; small tea growers also needed an affordable, lower-maintenance way to dry green tea leaves. To solve this, he built a steel-frame cooling bed with a built-in air duct and compressor that cools the sleeping surface, and separately developed a belt-driven tea dryer that reduces the tray-jamming and high maintenance of existing dryers.
